codeReview.summary: Timer tick integration into dispatch intents is implemented, but issue-assignment integration was not wired into production call paths and dedupe-key semantics do not allow issue_assigned intents to supersede timer hints for the same issue.

issues.description: Issue assignment still uses the legacy wakeup path instead of intent creation. `queueIssueAssignmentWakeup(...)` is still called here (also in `server/src/services/routines.ts:633`), and `queueIssueAssignmentWakeup` still calls `heartbeat.wakeup(...)` directly (`server/src/services/issue-assignment-wakeup.ts:45-46`). This does not satisfy the feature requirement to modify assignment wakeups to create `issue_assigned` dispatch intents.

The new assignment intent path uses `dedupeKey: assignment:${issueId}`, while timer intents use `dedupeKey: timer:${issueId}` (`server/src/services/timer-intent-bridge.ts:105`). Because keys differ, assignment-driven stronger intents cannot supersede timer hints for the same issue via deduplication, conflicting with VAL-HARD-008 intent supersession semantics (`validation-contract.md:123`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared-state docs do not define a canonical dedupeKey scheme across intent types for the same issue, which allows incompatible keys that break cross-type supersession behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md` documents deduplication conceptually but not key conventions; implementation now uses `timer:${issueId}` (`server/src/services/timer-intent-bridge.ts:105`) and `assignment:${issueId}` (`server/src/services/issue-assignment-wakeup.ts:91`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS.md requires company-scoped queries, but intent dedupe checks in intent-queue are not company-filtered.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission `AGENTS.md` states company-scoped multi-tenancy. In `server/src/services/intent-queue.ts:122-124` and `:165-166`, dedupe conditions only match on dedupeKey/status/priority without `companyId` filtering.

summary: Review failed with two blocking gaps: assignment wakeups remain on legacy `heartbeat.wakeup` call paths, and assignment/timer dedupe keys are incompatible for required timer supersession semantics. Review artifact written for scrutiny validator triage.
